articles.title: A Sub-2-Hour Marathon—and Second Place? The Bittersweet Triumph of Yomif Kejelcha
articles.summary: In his first marathon, Yomif Kejelcha shattered the 2-hour barrier with a 1:59:41—yet finished second. He calls it "crazy" and is thrilled, not bitter. His rival Sabastian Sawe set a new world record at 1:59:30.

articles.title: McMahon Grilled on Education Cuts: 'Spitting Mad' Parents and a Civil Rights Office in Crisis
articles.summary: In a tense hearing, Education Secretary Linda McMahon defended dismantling her department and cutting the Office for Civil Rights. Senators cited a disabled student's case dropped after office closures, while McMahon pledged to hire more lawyers.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz Standoff: Why a Faraway War Is Draining Your Wallet
articles.summary: As the US-Israel war with Iran hits day 60, the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, choking off 20% of global oil. Gas prices hit $4.18/gallon, inflation spikes, and peace talks are stalled. Experts warn of long-term economic pain.
